checks.name: README
checks.suggestion: Keep the first section clear: what it does, who it is for, and how to run it.

checks.name: License
checks.suggestion: MIT is a good default for small developer tools.

checks.name: .gitignore
checks.suggestion: Make sure it ignores virtual environments, cache files, build outputs, and secrets.

checks.name: Installation
checks.suggestion: Keep installation short and copy-paste friendly.

checks.name: Usage
checks.suggestion: Show the fastest possible example near the top of the README.

checks.name: Screenshots or media
checks.message: Screenshot, image, GIF, or visual media detected.
checks.suggestion: Use one strong screenshot or GIF near the top of the README.

checks.name: Demo link
checks.suggestion: Put the demo link near the top so visitors can try the project quickly.

checks.name: Badges
checks.suggestion: Use only useful badges: license, tests, package version, or deployment.

checks.name: Contributing guide
checks.suggestion: Keep contribution steps simple for beginners.

checks.name: Tests
checks.message: Test files or test directory detected.
checks.suggestion: Mention how to run tests in the README.

checks.name: CI workflow
checks.suggestion: Use CI to run tests automatically on every push.
